subject: DevOps
title: Control Plane and Worker Nodes
description: A fast breakdown of Control Plane and Worker Nodes for builders.

hook.text: A Kubernetes cluster has two types of machines: control plane nodes that decide, and worker nodes that execute.

buildup.text: The control plane is the brain. It stores desired state, schedules pods, and monitors the entire cluster.

discovery.text: Worker nodes do the heavy lifting. They run your containers, handle network traffic, and report status back.

twist.text: If a worker dies, the control plane reschedules its pods onto healthy nodes. Self-healing, automatic, no pages at 3 AM.

climax.text: Production clusters run multiple control plane nodes for high availability. If one fails, another takes over instantly.

punchline.text: Control plane decides. Workers execute. Together, they're a cluster.

quiz.question: What is the role of the control plane in Kubernetes?
options: It stores desired state, schedules pods, and monitors the cluster
It serves web traffic directly to users
